task_completion.description: The agent has navigated the CT volume to visualize the spleen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main viewport shows the abdominal cavity, specifically focusing on the Left Upper Quadrant (patient's left, viewer's right in standard radiology convention) where the spleen is located.

task_completion.description: The agent has switched to or is using the Coronal view for measurement.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The active viewport displays a Coronal plane (frontal view of the body), which is optimal for craniocaudal measurement. This is often indicated by the viewport label (e.g., 'Green' slice in Slicer) or the anatomical orientation.

task_completion.description: The agent uses the Markups ruler or line tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A ruler line, measurement annotation, or 'Markups' tool interface is visible and active on the screen.

task_completion.description: The measurement line is correctly placed on the spleen from superior to inferior pole.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A measurement line is drawn spanning the longest vertical (craniocaudal) dimension of the spleen. The line should be on the organ in the left upper quadrant, not on the liver (right side) or kidney.

task_completion.description: The measurement value in millimeters is visible on the screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A numeric value (e.g., '125.4 mm') is displayed next to the measurement line or in a 'Markups' table within the GUI.

task_completion.description: The agent generates and saves the JSON file with the required fields.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent is seen typing a command to save a JSON file (e.g., in a terminal) or editing a text file named 'splenic_measurement.json' containing fields 'length_mm', 'splenomegaly', and 'assessment'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the medical imaging software GUI to perform the measurement.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the medical viewer (e.g., 3D Slicer) with active tool overlays, mouse cursors, or menu selections, rather than just a terminal script running in the background.

integrity.description: The agent derived the value from the tool, not a pre-computed source.
integrity.visual_evidence: The value entered into the JSON file matches the value displayed by the measurement tool on the screen.

integrity.description: The measurement was performed in the image viewer, not estimated externally.
integrity.visual_evidence: The measurement line is physically drawn on the CT image data, not just estimated or typed into a text editor without visual reference.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The spleen is located in the Left Upper Quadrant of the abdomen (appearing on the right side of the image in standard radiological display). It is posterior and lateral to the stomach. In the AMOS dataset, the spleen label value is 1. Normal splenic length is <120mm. Splenomegaly is defined as >120mm for this task (Mild: 120-150mm, Marked: >150mm).
